verdict: AI displacement risk: moderate, with potential for automation in some tasks but not full replacement.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine Cognitive Tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Sound engineers will continue to work on tasks that require human judgment, such as mixing and mastering audio. However, some routine tasks like leveling and EQing can be automated.

reasoning_steps.title: Creative Judgment
reasoning_steps.body: While AI can process large amounts of data, it lacks the creative judgment and critical thinking skills required to make artistic decisions in sound engineering.

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory Moat
reasoning_steps.body: Sound engineers are protected by regulatory moats such as licensure requirements and industry standards that ensure a level of expertise and quality control.

adjacent_careers.name: Audio Engineer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Audio engineers will continue to work on tasks that require human judgment and creative decision-making, but may see some automation in routine tasks.

adjacent_careers.name: Music Producer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Music producers will need to adapt to AI-generated music and sound effects, but their creative judgment and critical thinking skills will remain valuable.

recent_developments.headline: AI-powered audio editing software gains popularity
recent_developments.impact: This development may lead to increased automation in routine audio editing tasks, but human sound engineers will still be needed for creative and high-end applications.

recent_developments.headline: AI coding assistants see broad enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Major firms report 25–30% of new code is now AI-generated and reviewed by engineers.

quotes.quote: Software engineering will be very different by the end of 2025. Most code will be written by AI, with human engineers reviewing and directing.
quotes.speaker: Dario Amodei
quotes.title: CEO, Anthropic

quotes.quote: AI is going to be the biggest technological shift of our lifetimes. Bigger than mobile, bigger than the web. Inside Microsoft, more than 30 percent of the code in some projects is now written by AI.
quotes.speaker: Satya Nadella
quotes.title: CEO, Microsoft

quotes.quote: More than a quarter of new code at Google is generated by AI, then reviewed and accepted by engineers.
quotes.speaker: Sundar Pichai
quotes.title: CEO, Google / Alphabet
